network: Human cancer signaling network (n=216 proteins, 1721 directed edges). Source: SIGNOR all_data_14_06_26.tsv, protein-protein only (UNIPROT), human (TAX_ID 9606 or species-agnostic), total_degree>=25 threshold. Represents directed regulatory/phosphorylation signals.
protocol: Same as DISC_D4_AI_v1 and DISC_OBSERVER_CELEGANS_v1: show edge list to 5 external AI systems in neutral sessions, ask which 20 proteins seem most important. Two conditions: named (real gene symbols) and anonymous (prt_NNN IDs).

network_properties.note: r(d1,d2)=-0.002: kinases (high d1) and transcription factors/targets (high d2) are nearly orthogonal structural classes, similar to npm consumer/infrastructure split.

d4_human.source: PubMed esearch: count of abstracts matching '"GENE"[tiab] AND cancer', queried 2026-06-14 via NCBI E-utilities API
d4_human.note: Human cancer attention is fame-driven: EGFR, KRAS, MET, BRCA1 are famous for mutation frequency and therapeutic targets, NOT structural centrality in signaling network. KRAS (pubmed=29005) has d2=2. MET (pubmed=58469) has d2=5.

baselines.note: r(d2,d4h)=+0.255 is POSITIVE, unlike npm (-0.077) and C. elegans (-0.044). Human cancer attention is moderately d2-aligned because several famous proteins (TP53 d2=42, MYC d2=23, STAT3 d2=28) happen to also be structural bottlenecks. But KRAS(d2=2), MET(d2=5), EGFR(d2=17) are famous with moderate-to-low d2, creating noise. H1 threshold is thus r(d2,d4_ai_anon)>0.255 (must exceed human partial alignment).

essential_ground_truth.definition: Proteins with d2>=19 (structural top quartile) AND pubmed_cancer<5000 (under-attended by human research attention). These are proteins that receive many regulatory signals but are NOT famous cancer drug targets or mutation hotspots. They represent the 'invisible load-bearing layer' of cancer signaling.
essential_ground_truth.contrast_set: Famous proteins with high pubmed but LOW d2: KRAS(d2=2,pubmed=29005), MET(d2=5,pubmed=58469), KIT(d2=9,pubmed=31351). AI anon should NOT recover these in top-20 if recovering by structure.

hypotheses.statement: r(d2, d4_ai_anon) > r(d2, d4_human): AI anonymous attention correlates MORE strongly with d2 than human PubMed cancer attention does. Baseline r(d2,d4h)=+0.255. Min threshold: r(d2,d4_ai_anon)>0.30 (must clearly exceed human partial alignment).

AI anon top-20 contains >=4 of 10 hidden infrastructure proteins (CTNNB1, ESR1, CDKN1B, MAPT, CDKN1A, IRS1, GSK3B, FOXO3, RELA, RAF1). Under hypergeometric null (N=216, K=10, n=20): expected=0.926, P(X>=3)=0.054, P(X>=4)=0.008, P(X>=5)=0.001. Confirm at k>=3 (p<0.054), strong at k>=4 (p<0.008).

Named condition reduces d2 recovery vs anonymous. Cancer gene names (KRAS, EGFR, BRCA1, HER2) carry strong fame priors. AI in named condition should rank famous mutation-hotspot genes high, reducing r(d2). Expected to be MOST PRONOUNCED of all 3 OPL domains because cancer gene names are maximally fame-loaded in AI training data.
hypotheses.prediction: r(d2, d4_ai_named) < r(d2, d4_ai_anon) by >=0.10 for most models

hypotheses.statement: Famous low-d2 proteins (KRAS, MET, KIT) should appear in named top-20 but NOT in anon top-20. This directly tests whether structural vs fame projection produces different rankings for the same proteins.
hypotheses.prediction: KRAS/MET/KIT appear in named top-20 of >=3 models but in anon top-20 of <=1 model

opl_context.note: This is the 3rd domain for the Observer Projection Law. npm (software, human r_d2=-0.077) + C.elegans (neuroscience, human r_d2=-0.044) + Cancer signaling (human r_d2=+0.255). Three domains spanning engineered systems, simple nervous systems, and disease biology. Cancer has POSITIVE human baseline unlike the other two, making the structural contrast richer: AI tracks d2 MORE efficiently than human attention even when human attention already shows partial d2 alignment.
